---
title: "The Evolution of Geographical Organizations in India: A Historical Perspective"
date: "2026-02-19"
categories: [History, Geography, Institutional Growth, India]
image: "social/institutions_list.png"
description: "Tracing the journey of geographical societies in India from the early 20th century to the modern geospatial era."
---

Geography as an academic discipline in India has seen a remarkable evolution, transitioning from a colonial descriptive tool to a high-tech spatial science. Central to this journey has been the birth and growth of various geographical organizations and societies. These bodies have not only provided a platform for researchers but have also shaped the curriculum and policy advocacy for the discipline (National Association of Geographers, India [NAGI], 2024; Shodhganga, 2020).

## The Early Foundations (1920s – 1940s)

The formal institutionalization of Geography in India began in the early 1920s. The **[Indian Geographical Society (IGS)](http://igschennai.org/)**, established in **1926** in Chennai (as the Madras Geographical Association), holds the distinction of being the oldest (Indian Geographical Society, 2024). Founded by **Prof. N. Subrahmanyam**, it paved the way for professional geographical discourse in South Asia (University of Madras, 2023).

Soon after, the **[Geographical Society of India (GSI)](http://geographicalsocietyofindia.org.in/)** was founded on July 29, **1933**, in Kolkata (initially as the Calcutta Geographical Society) by a group of thirteen founding members (Geographical Society of India, 2019). Following independence, the **[National Geographical Society of India (NGSI)](http://ngsi.in/)** was established in **1946** at Banaras Hindu University (BHU), Varanasi, by **Prof. Harbans Lal Chhibbar**, marking a new chapter in post-colonial geographical research (National Geographical Society of India, 2024; Wikipedia, 2024).

## The Growth of National & Specialized Bodies (1960s – 1980s)

As the discipline matured, the need for a national umbrella organization led to the establishment of the **[National Association of Geographers, India (NAGI)](https://sites.google.com/view/nagiorgin/home)** in **1978** (NAGI, 2024). Based at the Delhi School of Economics, NAGI has since been the primary coordinator for the annual Indian Geography Congress.

This period also saw the rise of specialized societies:
*   **Indian Society of Remote Sensing (ISRS)** (1969): Bridging geography with space technology (Indian Society of Remote Sensing, 2025).
*   **[Association of Population Geographers of India](http://apgin.org/)** (1978): Focusing on the demographic complexities of India (Association of Population Geographers of India, 2023).
*   **[Institute of Indian Geographers (IIG)](https://unipune.ac.in/dept/science/geography/default.htm)**, Pune (1979): Promoting rigorous scientific research across all sub-fields (Institute of Indian Geographers, 2024).

## Directory of Geographical Institutions in India (Verified 2024–25)

The table below lists major geographical associations, their current representatives (verified for the 2024–25 term), and their historical host institutions.

| Name of the Association/Society | Estd. | Host Institution | Current Representative(s) |
|:---|:---:|:---|:---|
| **[NAGI (National Association)](https://sites.google.com/view/nagiorgin/home)** | 1978 | Delhi School of Economics | **Prof. Salahuddin Qureshi** (President), **Prof. A K Saha** (Secy. General) |
| **[IGS (Chennai)](http://igschennai.org/)** | 1926 | University of Madras | Prof. N. Sivagnanam (President), Prof. R. Jaganathan (Gen Secy) |
| **[GSI (Kolkata)](http://geographicalsocietyofindia.org.in/)** | 1933 | University of Calcutta | Smt. Suprova Ray (President), Prof. Apurba Rabi Ghosh (Hony. Secy) |
| **[NGSI (Varanasi)](http://ngsi.in/)** | 1946 | BHU, Varanasi | **Professor V. N. Sharma** (Hony. Secretary) |
| **[IIG (Pune)](https://unipune.ac.in/dept/science/geography/default.htm)** | 1979 | Pune University | **Prof. D. K. Nayak** (President), **Prof. Ravindra G. Jaybhaye** (Secy General) |
| **[APGI (Chandigarh)](http://apgin.org/)** | 1978 | Panjab University | **Prof. Krishna Mohan** (President), **Dr. Gaurav Kalotra** (Secretary) |
| **[ILEE (Kolkata)](http://ilee.org.in/)** | 1983 | Kolkata | Prof. L. N. Satpati (Director) |
| **[IGI (Geomorphologists)](http://www.indiageomorph.org/)** | 1988 | University of Allahabad | Prof. Sunil Kumar De (President) |
| **[Deccan Geographical Society](http://dgsi.org.in/)** | 1962 | SP Pune University | Prof. B. C. Vaidya (Executive) |
| **[NEIGS (Guwahati)](http://neigs.org.in/)** | 1968 | Gauhati University | Prof. Abani K. Bhagabati & Prof. Bimal K. Kar |
| **[MBP (Maharashtra)](http://mbpune.org.in/)** | 1984 | Pune | Prof. Praveen Saptarshi |
| **[ISG (Geomatics)](http://isgindia.org/)** | 1993 | ISRO-SAC, Ahmedabad | Executive Committee 2024-25 |
| **[Aligarh Geographical Society](https://www.amu.ac.in/department/geography)** | 1924* | AMU, Aligarh | Prof. Ateeque Ahmed |
| **[Allahabad Geog. Society](https://allduniv.ac.in/department-of-geography)** | - | Allahabad University | Prof. Manorama Sinha |
| **[AGBJ (Bihar/JH)](http://agbj.org.in/)** | - | Patna University | Prof. Devender Prasad Singh |
| **[UGIT (Bengaluru)](http://ugit.net.in/)** | 2000s | Bangalore University | Prof. Ashok Hanjagi |
| **[Uttar Bharat Bhoogol Parishad](http://ubbp.org/)** | - | Gorakhpur | Prof. V. K. Srivatsava |
| **[RGA (Rajasthan)](http://rga.ac.in/)** | - | Bhilwara | Dr. S. S. Bhat |
| **[GAG (Goa)](http://geographersassociationgoa.blogspot.com/)** | - | Goa | Prof. Prabirkumar Rath |
| **[Himalayan Samiksha Parishad](http://www.himparishad.org/)** | - | Kolkata | Prof. Sunando Banarjee |
| **[Indian Society of Spatial Scientists](http://spatialscientists.org/)** | 2010 | Kolkata | Prof. Ashis Sarkar |
| **[Konkan Geographers Association](http://konkangeographers.org/)** | - | Sindhudurg | Prof. R.B. Patil |

*\*Years marked with asterisks denote early informal society formations or departmental origins.*

## Modern Era: Geospatial Expansion & GIS

In the 21st century, organizations like the **Association of Geospatial Industries (AGI)** and **Indian Society of Geomatics (ISG)** have taken the lead in integrating satellite-based remote sensing, LiDAR, and GIS into the geographical framework (AGI, 2024; ISG, 2024). These organizations foster collaboration between the industry and academia, ensuring that Indian geographers are at the forefront of the global spatial revolution.

The recent participation of Indian representatives in the **Asian Geographical Association (founded 2018)**, where Prof. Suresh Chand Rai represented NAGI, further exemplifies the global reach of Indian geographical research (Asian Geographical Association, 2024; Geo-DOI, 2018).

## 📅 Upcoming Geographical Events (Post-March 2026)

Staying connected with the evolving spatial sciences is vital. Here are the key upcoming events scheduled for late 2026 and 2027:

*   **XXII INQUA Congress 2027 India**
    *   *Theme:* Quaternary Science As Societal Services
    *   *Dates:* January 28 – February 3, 2027
    *   *Location:* Lucknow, India
    *   *Note:* The largest global congress for Quaternary sciences with 188 scientific sessions.
*   **Western Regional Conference: IASP 2026**
    *   *Theme:* Demographic Transition in Western India
    *   *Dates:* March 20-21, 2026
    *   *Host:* Goa Business School, Goa University

## ✅ Recently Concluded Events

The geographical community has seen several landmark meets in 2024-2025 and early 2026:

*   **3rd Indian Quaternary Congress 2026:** Held from January 19-23, 2026.
*   **47th Indian Geography Congress (NAGI) 2025:** Hosted by University of Rajasthan, Jaipur (Nov 13-15, 2025).
*   **ISG-ISRS National Symposium 2025:** Focusing on advances in Geomatics (Nov 25-27, 2025).
*   **46th IIG Annual Meet & International Conference 2025:** Held at HNBGU, Uttarakhand (Oct 25-27, 2025).
*   **9th NAGI International Conference 2025:** Ranchi University, Ranchi (Oct 7-9, 2025).
*   **46th Indian Geography Congress (NAGI) 2024:** Osmania University, Hyderabad (Dec 27-29, 2024).
*   **45th IIG Annual Meet:** NEHU, Shillong (Dec 19-21, 2024).

## Conclusion

From the local "Madras Geographical Association" of 1926 to the high-tech geospatial societies of today, geographical organizations in India have been the pillars of the discipline. They continue to play a crucial role in addressing national challenges—from urban planning and disaster management to climate change analysis—demonstrating that Geography is not just about mapping the world, but about understanding and improving it.

## References

Aligarh Muslim University. (n.d.). *Department of Geography: Our Heritage*. https://www.amu.ac.in/department/geography

Asian Geographical Association. (2024). *Founding committee and member countries*. http://asiageo.net

Association of Population Geographers of India. (2023). *Executive body term 2023-2025*. http://apgin.org

Foundation of Practising Geographer. (n.d.). *Evolution of geographical discourse in India*. http://practicing-geographer.org

Geo-DOI Archive. (2018). *Report on the formation of Asian Geographical Association in 2018*. http://geodoi.ac.in

Geographical Society of India. (2019). *Founding principles and history*. http://geographicalsocietyofindia.org.in

Indian Geographical Society. (2024). *History of IGS Chennai*. http://igschennai.org

Indian Society of Remote Sensing. (2025). *About ISRS: History and objectives*. https://isrsindia.in

Institute of Indian Geographers. (2024). *Constitution and executive council 2024-25*. https://iigeo.org

National Association of Geographers, India. (2024). *Official lists: Executive committee 2024-25*. https://sites.google.com/view/nagiorgin/home

National Geographical Society of India. (2024). *Publications and secretary journals*. http://ngsi.in

Shodhganga. (2020). *Institutional framework for geography in India*. http://inflibnet.ac.in

University of Madras. (2023). *Department of Geography: Research & societies*. https://unom.ac.in

Wikipedia contributors. (2024). *National Geographical Society of India*. Wikipedia, The Free Encyclopedia. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Geographical_Society_of_India
